title: TASK-013 QA red
description:
## Task: TASK-013 Geometry helpers

Role: QA
Authoritative docs: docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (Phase B / TASK-013), docs/05_module_sdk.md (Geometry Helpers), docs/08_coordinate_system.md (The Rule)
Files to create/modify:
- crates/slicer-core/tests/geometry_helpers_tdd.rs (create)
- crates/slicer-core/Cargo.toml (modify only for test dependencies if required)

Context:
Write the red tests for reusable geometry helper APIs in crates/slicer-core before any implementation starts. Cover the helper surface promised by the docs: segment_path, distribute_points, path_length, seg_len_3d, and flow_correction. OrcaSlicerDocumented is not present in this workspace, so there are no upstream Orca reference files to cite for this task.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo test -p slicer-core geometry_helpers_tdd fails for the expected missing or incorrect helper behavior before implementation
- Tests exercise happy path, empty or degenerate input, boundary values, and finite-output assertions for the helper APIs named above
- At least one property-style test covers a geometry invariant required by the Phase B quality gate
- Tests follow docs/08_coordinate_system.md and avoid raw Orca scaling assumptions

TDD requirement:
Write the tests first and prove they are red with cargo test -p slicer-core geometry_helpers_tdd.

title: TASK-013 coding green
description:
## Task: TASK-013 Geometry helpers

Role: Coding
Authoritative docs: docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (Phase B / TASK-013), docs/05_module_sdk.md (Geometry Helpers), docs/08_coordinate_system.md (named epsilon constants)
Files to create/modify:
- crates/slicer-core/src/geometry.rs (create)
- crates/slicer-core/src/lib.rs (modify to export geometry helpers)
- crates/slicer-core/Cargo.toml (modify only if dependencies are required by the approved red tests)
- crates/slicer-core/tests/geometry_helpers_tdd.rs (modify only as needed to keep the red tests authoritative)

Context:
Implement the geometry helper primitives promised by the architecture and SDK docs after the QA red tests exist. Keep the scope to deterministic reusable helpers needed by later tasks: path segmentation, point distribution, path length, 3D segment length, flow correction, and any named epsilon constants required by docs/08_coordinate_system.md. OrcaSlicerDocumented is not present in this workspace, so there are no upstream Orca reference files to reuse directly.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo test -p slicer-core passes
- Helper names and behavior match the documented API examples in docs/05_module_sdk.md
- Coordinate conversion and tolerances follow docs/08_coordinate_system.md; no raw 1_000_000-scale Orca constants appear
- Public items added for the helper API have rustdoc comments and deterministic behavior for degenerate inputs
- No implementation work starts until the QA task has produced failing tests

TDD requirement:
Implement only after the QA task lands red tests, then make those tests green without expanding scope beyond TASK-013.

title: TASK-013 docs/status
description:
## Task: TASK-013 Geometry helpers

Role: Docs
Authoritative docs: docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (Docs Agent), docs/07_implementation_status.md (Phase B)
Files to create/modify:

Context:
After QA and coding both verify TASK-013, update implementation tracking and ensure newly exposed public geometry helpers are documented consistently with the architecture docs.

Acceptance criteria:
- TASK-013 is marked complete in docs/07_implementation_status.md only after cargo test -p slicer-core passes
- Any architecture deviation is recorded in docs/DEVIATION_LOG.md instead of silently rewriting normative docs
- Public geometry helper items added by TASK-013 have rustdoc comments aligned with the implemented API surface

TDD requirement:
Do not mark docs complete until the QA red and coding green tasks are both verified.

title: Prepare TASK-013 task chain
description: Read docs, inspect slicer-core and Orca references, then enqueue the TASK-013 QA, coding, and docs subagent tasks with correct dependencies.

title: TASK-014 QA red
description: Role: QA. Authoritative docs: ./docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (TASK-014), ./docs/01_system_architecture.md (Host Services; Data Ownership Rules), ./docs/03_wit_and_manifest.md (mesh query host services), ./docs/00_project_overview.md (Repository Structure), ./docs/08_coordinate_system.md (coordinate scaling when adapting upstream geometry). Files to create or modify: crates/slicer-core/tests/aabb_tree_tdd.rs (create), crates/slicer-core/src/lib.rs (modify only to expose temporary todo stubs needed for the red suite to compile), and any new slicer-core source module declarations required for the test target to compile. Context: define the public AABB-tree-for-mesh-queries contract for slicer-core before implementation so later coding can build accelerated raycast, bounds, and nearest-query helpers without drifting from the architecture docs. OrcaSlicer references to inspect and cite while designing the tests: OrcaSlicerDocumented/tests/libslic3r/test_aabbindirect.cpp, OrcaSlicerDocumented/tests/libslic3r/test_indexed_triangle_set.cpp, OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/AABBMesh.cpp, and OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/AABBTreeIndirect.hpp. Acceptance criteria: cargo test -p slicer-core --test aabb_tree_tdd compiles and fails against explicit todo or unimplemented stubs for the new helper API; the red suite covers deterministic tree construction over slicer_ir::IndexedTriangleSet plus at least first-hit ray query, all-hits ray query, and nearest-point or squared-distance behavior on a simple closed mesh; all coordinates and expectations use ModularSlicer mm and Point helpers rather than raw scaled literals; no production implementation beyond temporary stubs lands in this task. TDD requirement: write the tests first and make them fail before any real implementation begins.

title: TASK-014 coding green
description: Role: Coding. Authoritative docs: ./docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (TASK-014), ./docs/01_system_architecture.md (Host Services; Data Ownership Rules), ./docs/03_wit_and_manifest.md (mesh query host services), ./docs/00_project_overview.md (Repository Structure), and ./docs/08_coordinate_system.md (coordinate scaling). Files to create or modify: crates/slicer-core/src/lib.rs, crates/slicer-core/src/aabb_tree.rs (expected new module unless the existing structure strongly justifies another single module), and any focused crate-local unit coverage needed inside slicer-core; do not expand beyond TASK-014 surface area. Context: implement the AABB tree and mesh-query helpers defined by the red suite so ModularSlicer can support fast host-side mesh raycasts, bounds checks, and nearest geometric lookups without exposing mesh data across the WASM boundary. Reuse ideas from OrcaSlicerDocumented/tests/libslic3r/test_aabbindirect.cpp, OrcaSlicerDocumented/tests/libslic3r/test_indexed_triangle_set.cpp, OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/AABBMesh.cpp, and OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/AABBTreeIndirect.hpp, but adapt them to the documented Rust crate structure and ModularSlicer coordinate conventions. Acceptance criteria: the new red suite passes; cargo test -p slicer-core passes; exported APIs stay limited to the TASK-014 mesh-query helper surface defined by QA; implementation remains deterministic and read-only over IndexedTriangleSet input data; no architecture-doc deviations are introduced.

title: TASK-014 docs/status
description: Role: Docs. Authoritative docs: ./docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (TASK-014) and ./docs/07_implementation_status.md. Files to create or modify: docs/07_implementation_status.md only, unless a documented architecture deviation must also be recorded in docs/DEVIATION_LOG.md. Context: once TASK-014 code is implemented and verified, record completion in the authoritative status tracker without changing unrelated checklist items. Acceptance criteria: re-run the TASK-014 verification commands documented by QA or Coding, confirm the AABB tree implementation is limited to the intended slicer-core mesh-query scope, mark TASK-014 complete in docs/07_implementation_status.md, and leave later tasks untouched; if any deviation from the architecture docs was required, record it before marking the task complete.

title: TASK-015 QA red
description: Role: QA. Authoritative docs: docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md TASK-015, docs/01_system_architecture.md Layer::SlicePostProcess and paint propagation contract, docs/02_ir_schemas.md PaintRegionIR/SliceIR ExPolygon and Polygon, docs/05_module_sdk.md host point_in_paint_region and segment_in_paint_region, docs/08_coordinate_system.md. Files to create or modify: crates/slicer-core/tests/point_in_polygon_tdd.rs, crates/slicer-core/src/point_in_polygon.rs, crates/slicer-core/src/lib.rs. Context: define the red contract for deterministic point-in-polygon classification used by PaintRegionAnnotator and host point_in_paint_region against paint polygons including holes and boundary semantics. Orca references to inspect and cite in the implementation brief: OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/ExPolygon.cpp lines 182-205 for contour-plus-hole contains semantics, OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/AABBTreeIndirect.hpp lines 992-1019 for candidate filtering concepts, OrcaSlicerDocumented/tests/libslic3r/test_polygon.cpp and test_geometry.cpp for polygon containment coverage. Acceptance criteria: cargo test -p slicer-core --test point_in_polygon_tdd compiles and fails on explicit todo stubs before implementation; tests cover empty input, simple polygon containment independent of winding, hole exclusion, deterministic boundary behavior, and paint-region precedence hooks needed later by host point_in_paint_region; no implementation beyond minimal public stubs.

title: TASK-015 coding green
description: Role: Coding. Authoritative docs: docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md TASK-015, docs/01_system_architecture.md Layer::SlicePostProcess plus paint annotation failure semantics, docs/02_ir_schemas.md PaintRegionIR and ExPolygon contracts, docs/05_module_sdk.md host point_in_paint_region and segment_in_paint_region, docs/08_coordinate_system.md. Files to create or modify: crates/slicer-core/src/point_in_polygon.rs, crates/slicer-core/src/lib.rs, and only minimal crate-local unit coverage if required. Context: implement exactly the API established by the red suite for deterministic point-in-polygon queries over paint regions so later host annotation can classify contour points without deviating from IR geometry contracts. Orca references to inspect and cite: OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/ExPolygon.cpp lines 182-205, OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/AABBTreeIndirect.hpp lines 992-1019, OrcaSlicerDocumented/tests/libslic3r/test_polygon.cpp, OrcaSlicerDocumented/tests/libslic3r/test_geometry.cpp. Acceptance criteria: cargo test -p slicer-core --test point_in_polygon_tdd passes; cargo test -p slicer-core passes; implementation preserves hole handling and boundary semantics deterministically, uses ModularSlicer scaled-int Point2 conventions, and does not expand scope beyond TASK-015.

title: TASK-015 docs/status
description: Role: Docs. Authoritative docs: docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md implementation ordering and docs step, docs/07_implementation_status.md, plus the verified TASK-015 contract from docs/01_system_architecture.md, docs/02_ir_schemas.md, docs/05_module_sdk.md, and docs/08_coordinate_system.md. Files to create or modify: docs/07_implementation_status.md only unless a deviation must be recorded separately. Context: after TASK-015 code is green and reverified, mark Point-in-polygon for paint region queries complete without changing architecture docs. Acceptance criteria: rerun cargo test -p slicer-core --test point_in_polygon_tdd and cargo test -p slicer-core successfully before updating status; docs/07_implementation_status.md marks TASK-015 complete; commit is docs-only.

title: TASK-020 QA red
description:
## Task: TASK-020 Manifest ingestion

Role: QA
Authoritative docs: ./docs/03_wit_and_manifest.md (Module Manifest Schema), ./docs/04_host_scheduler.md (Phase 1 - Manifest Ingestion), ./docs/01_system_architecture.md (module discovery precedence), ./docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (TASK-020)
Files to create/modify:
- Cargo.toml (modify: add crates/slicer-host workspace member if absent)
- crates/slicer-host/Cargo.toml (create)
- crates/slicer-host/src/lib.rs (create)
- crates/slicer-host/src/manifest.rs (create: public stubs only)
- crates/slicer-host/tests/manifest_ingestion_tdd.rs (create)
Context:
Define the red contract for host manifest ingestion in crates/slicer-host. OrcaSlicerDocumented inspection found no reusable module-manifest scheduler artifacts; the only manifest file is the unrelated Windows app manifest at OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/dev-utils/platform/msw/OrcaSlicer.manifest.in, so follow ModularSlicer docs directly.
Acceptance criteria:
- cargo test -p slicer-host --test manifest_ingestion_tdd compiles and fails only on explicit todo!/stub behavior
- Tests lock down TOML parsing into the documented LoadedModule runtime surface, manifest kebab-case to runtime snake_case normalization, sibling wasm pairing, unknown stage rejection, root-precedence duplicate handling, and PostPass::LayerFinalization effective layer_parallel_safe=false normalization
- Structured load errors include manifest path and field/context for schema failures
- No production implementation beyond minimal public stubs needed to compile the red suite
TDD requirement:
Write tests in crates/slicer-host/tests before implementing ingestion.

title: TASK-020 coding green
description:
## Task: TASK-020 Manifest ingestion

Role: Coding
Authoritative docs: ./docs/03_wit_and_manifest.md (Module Manifest Schema, layer-parallel-safe contract, WIT/IR compatibility matrix), ./docs/04_host_scheduler.md (Phase 1 - Manifest Ingestion, LoadedModule naming map), ./docs/01_system_architecture.md (module search roots and duplicate precedence), ./docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (TASK-020)
Files to create/modify:
- Cargo.toml (modify: add crates/slicer-host workspace member if absent)
- crates/slicer-host/Cargo.toml
- crates/slicer-host/src/lib.rs
- crates/slicer-host/src/manifest.rs
- crates/slicer-host/tests/manifest_ingestion_tdd.rs (only if tiny test-fix adjustments are required)
Context:
Implement the crates/slicer-host manifest ingestion contract defined by the red suite without expanding into DAG construction or runtime execution. OrcaSlicerDocumented has no relevant module-manifest ingestion implementation to reuse; ignore the unrelated Windows app manifest file and follow the docs exactly. If compatibility metadata from docs/03 is needed beyond the documented LoadedModule fields in docs/04, keep any extra representation crate-private rather than changing the normative runtime surface.
Acceptance criteria:
- cargo test -p slicer-host --test manifest_ingestion_tdd passes
- cargo test -p slicer-host passes
- Ingestion normalizes manifest naming and search-root precedence exactly as documented, rejects unknown stages and missing sibling wasm files, and forces effective layer_parallel_safe=false for PostPass::LayerFinalization modules
- Structured load errors remain deterministic and include manifest path plus field/context for schema failures
- No scope creep into TASK-021+ APIs or execution machinery
TDD requirement:
Implement only after the red tests exist and fail.

title: TASK-020 docs/status
description:
## Task: TASK-020 Manifest ingestion

Role: Docs
Authoritative docs: ./docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (TASK-020), ./docs/07_implementation_status.md, plus the TASK-020 validation evidence from ./docs/03_wit_and_manifest.md and ./docs/04_host_scheduler.md
Files to create/modify:
- docs/07_implementation_status.md
Context:
After TASK-020 red and green work is complete, revalidate the dedicated slicer-host tests and mark Manifest ingestion complete in the implementation status document.
Acceptance criteria:
- cargo test -p slicer-host --test manifest_ingestion_tdd passes
- cargo test -p slicer-host passes
- docs/07_implementation_status.md marks TASK-020 Manifest ingestion complete and no unrelated status lines change
- Commit contains only the docs/status update
TDD requirement:
Do not mark the task complete until the red suite existed first and the green implementation has already landed.

title: TASK-027 QA red
description: Authoritative docs: docs/01_system_architecture.md (Tier 1 PrePass, data dependency matrix), docs/04_host_scheduler.md (PrePass execution, error handling, fixed stage order), docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (QA role, TDD cycle), docs/10_glossary_and_scenario_traces.md (Blackboard/global layer invariants). Files: crates/slicer-host/tests/prepass_executor_tdd.rs create; crates/slicer-host/src/prepass.rs create stub API if needed; crates/slicer-host/src/lib.rs modify exports if needed. Context: define the red API and tests for TASK-027 so execute_prepass runs compiled prepass stages sequentially, commits outputs into Blackboard exactly once, preserves fixed stage order, and aborts deterministically on module/prepass contract failures before per-layer work. Acceptance: cargo test -p slicer-host --test prepass_executor_tdd compiles and fails only on the TASK-027 todo stub; the suite locks down stage ordering, prepass slot commit behavior for SurfaceClassificationIR/LayerPlanIR/PaintRegionIR/RegionMapIR, duplicate-commit rejection, missing dependency/error propagation, and immutable Blackboard reuse of mesh input. Orca references checked: no direct modular executor exists, but sequencing context is relevant in OrcaSlicerDocumented/generated_documentation/01_system_architecture.md and OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/PrintObject.cpp. TDD requirement: write failing tests first and keep implementation as explicit todo stubs only.

title: TASK-027 coding green
description: Blocked by TASK-027 QA red. Implement the TASK-027 prepass executor API to satisfy the red suite once it exists, following docs/01_system_architecture.md, docs/04_host_scheduler.md, and docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md. Files expected: crates/slicer-host/src/prepass.rs, crates/slicer-host/src/lib.rs, and any narrowly scoped supporting test fixtures. Acceptance: cargo test -p slicer-host --test prepass_executor_tdd passes and cargo test -p slicer-host stays green. Orca references: OrcaSlicerDocumented/generated_documentation/01_system_architecture.md plus OrcaSlicerDocumented/src/libslic3r/PrintObject.cpp for high-level sequencing only.

Acceptance: cargo test -p slicer-host --test prepass_executor_tdd passes, cargo test -p slicer-host passes, and docs/07_implementation_status.md records TASK-027 complete with no undocumented deviations.

title: TASK-029 QA red
description: Role: QA. Authoritative docs: docs/01_system_architecture.md PrePass PaintSegmentation, docs/02_ir_schemas.md PaintRegionIR, docs/04_host_scheduler.md prepass flow, docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md TASK-029, docs/10_glossary_and_scenario_traces.md paint-heavy trace. Files: create crates/slicer-host/tests/paint_segmentation_executor_tdd.rs and crates/slicer-host/src/paint_segmentation.rs; modify crates/slicer-host/src/lib.rs. Acceptance: add failing tests first for execute_paint_segmentation from Arc MeshIR plus SurfaceClassificationIR plus LayerPlanIR to PaintRegionIR, covering deterministic per-layer semantic grouping, authoritative global-layer indexing, paint_order preservation for same-semantic overlaps, and stable empty-scene behavior; cargo test -p slicer-host --test paint_segmentation_executor_tdd must fail only at the explicit todo stub, and cargo test -p slicer-host -- --skip paint_segmentation_executor_tdd must stay green. SubAgent role: QA.

title: TASK-030 QA red
description: Role: QA. Authoritative docs: docs/01_system_architecture.md (Tier 2 Layer::SlicePostProcess and Paint Annotation Failure Semantics), docs/04_host_scheduler.md (Phase 4 per-layer execution ordering), docs/06_agent_implementation_guide.md (TDD cycle + QA role), docs/09_progress_events.md (module_error degraded/fatal event contract), and docs/10_glossary_and_scenario_traces.md (Scenario Trace 2 and glossary invariants for boundary_paint). OrcaSlicerDocumented references: src/libslic3r/MultiMaterialSegmentation.cpp, src/libslic3r/MultiMaterialSegmentation.hpp, src/libslic3r/PrintObject.cpp project_triangles_to_slabs(), and src/libslic3r/TriangleSelector.hpp for upstream paint projection/annotation context. Files: create crates/slicer-host/tests/slice_postprocess_paint_annotation_tdd.rs, create crates/slicer-host/src/slice_postprocess.rs, and modify crates/slicer-host/src/lib.rs to export the API stub only. Acceptance: cargo test -p slicer-host --test slice_postprocess_paint_annotation_tdd must compile and fail only at the explicit TASK-030 todo stub; the rest of slicer-host should stay green when the new red test is skipped. SubAgent role: QA.

title: TASK-030 coding green
